items.claim: The review flags STORY-CONSUME as a phantom consumer because FILE-SEARCH-API has no approved producer or upstream artifact.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the review identifies the phantom consumer, cites whole-graph/source closure, names STORY-CONSUME and FILE-SEARCH-API, and says the plan should have stopped.

items.claim: The review flags the STORY-ORDER to STORY-CONSUME dependency as unsupported by the approved sequencing source.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the review names the unsupported edge, cites dependency graph validity and SEQ-001, and treats the defect as a stop-worthy blocker.

items.claim: The review flags prose-only evidence because the defective plan says tests exist instead of binding evidence to the concrete pnpm check gate.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the review distinguishes vague evidence from the named VAL-001 gate and identifies the missing concrete command or check.

items.claim: The review flags runtime cost within budget as an unsourced operand and stop-condition violation.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the review names runtime cost and budget as unsupported values and ties the issue to predicate/operand sourcing or STOP-001.

items.claim: The review flags packages/plan-schema/src/index.ts, JSON Schema, and TypeScript interface as forbidden Jig schema invention.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the review treats schema/interface/package creation as a scope blocker, not as useful implementation detail.

items.claim: The review flags stop-condition drift because the plan emitted work despite phantom consumers, unsupported edges, prose evidence, or unsourced values.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the review says the defective plan should have stopped rather than merely requesting follow-up cleanup.

items.claim: The review is disposition-ready: each blocker is independently actionable, source-cited, and severe enough for a reviewer to reject the plan.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the review gives clear defect labels, violated requirements, source refs, and concrete remediation posture without accepting the defective plan.
